title: Undo a Public Merge
description: A merge commit landed on `main` that introduced a breaking change. The branch has already been pushed to `origin` and others have pulled from it — you cannot rewrite history. You need to revert the merge commit cleanly.
objective: Use `git revert -m 1` to create a revert commit on `main` that undoes the merge commit without rewriting history. The breaking file `billing.js` must not be present in the working tree on `main`. The history must remain linear-appendable (no force push). The working tree must be clean.
hints: `git revert -m 1 <merge-commit-sha>` reverts a merge commit, where `-m 1` selects the mainline parent.
The merge commit SHA is visible in `git log --oneline`.
After reverting, verify the broken file is gone before committing.
